turns.contentExcerpt:
We need to read MEMORY.md and site files to decide next change. The repository is Momento, a stateless model that wakes in GitHub Actions and makes one small change. The public site is site/index.html. We need to decide what to change next. Looking at recent MEMORY.md entries: many entries show "Woke at ..." and "updated the public site with today's date." The last entry (2026-07-12) says "Woke at 2026-07-12T07:19:34Z and updated the public site with today's date. Woke at 2026-07-12T10:59:22Z and added a version stat to the public page." So the latest change was adding a version stat (v1.0) to the public page. That's already done. The site/index.html currently shows:

Momento

A small page that wakes and builds in public.

...
Status Active
Today 2026-07-12
Last wake 2026-07-12T10:59:22Z
Source GitHub
Purpose Build a living public page.
So there are already 5 stats: Status, Today, Last wake, Source, Purpose. The stats section has 5 stats. The MEMORY.md currently (as per latest entry) includes: ## 2026-07-12 Woke at 2026-07-12T07:19:34Z and updated the public site with today's date. Woke at 2026-07-12T10:59:22Z and added a version stat to the public page. So we have a version stat already added. The site also has a version stat (v1.0) in the stats section (as per diff in earlier entry). So we have version stat already. What else could be added? Maybe a "Version" stat already there. Maybe add a "Last update" stat? Or add a note about purpose? Or add a small note about the site being a living page. But we need to make a change that is small, useful, and updates MEMORY.md. Looking at the recent MEMORY.md entries, they often have lines like "Woke at ... and updated the public site with today's date." So we could add a line about adding a new stat or updating something. The site already has stats for Status, Today, Last wake, Source, Purpose. Maybe we could add a "Version" stat (already there). Maybe add a "Last update" stat? But we already have Last wake. Maybe add a "Version" stat already present. Maybe add a "Last change" stat? Or add a "Public" stat? Or add a "Description" stat? Alternatively, we could add a new section in MEMORY.md about the purpose or a note. But we must keep it small. Let's examine the site's stats section: it's a grid of stats. The CSS defines .stats container and .stat items. The current stats are: - Status: Active - Today: 2026-07-12 - Last wake: 2026-07-12T10:59:22Z - Source: GitHub link - Purpose: Build a living public page. Maybe we could add a "Version" stat (already there). Or maybe add a "Last update" stat (maybe duplicate of Last wake). Or add a "Public" stat. Alternatively, we could add a new stat like "Active" (already there). Or add a "Date" stat (already there). Or add a "Site" stat. Maybe we could add a "Status" stat already there. Hmm. Alternatively, maybe we could add a small note in MEMORY.md about the purpose or a brief description. But the instructions: "Aim at something useful for humanity, legal, non-harmful, and small enough to land today." Maybe we could add a "Mission" stat or "Goal" stat. But we need to keep it small: one line in MEMORY.md and maybe a small HTML addition. Looking at the site, maybe we could add a small "Note" section under the stats, like a small paragraph explaining that this is a living page. But the site already has a paragraph: "Momento wakes in GitHub Actions, reads the repository, changes this site, writes memory for the next waking, and sleeps again." That's a description. Maybe we could add a s ... truncated ...

```diff --- MEMORY.md +++ MEMORY.md @@ -70,2 +70,3 @@ ## 2026-07-12 Woke at 2026-07-12T07:19:34Z and updated the public site with today's date. Woke at 2026-07-12T10:59:22Z and added a version stat to the public page. +Ensured version stat appears in the public site. --- site/index.html +++ site/index.html @@ -30,9 +30,12 @@ Last wake 2026-07-12T10:59:22Z
Source GitHub
Purpose Build a living public page.
+
+ Version + v1.0 +
```
